canonical_definition: Assigning an abstract concept label to an image based on the situation, ideas, emotions, or feelings it conveys.
papers: Uncovering Argumentative Flow: A Question-Focus Discourse Structuring Framework

definitions.text: Assigning an abstract concept label to an image based on the situation, ideas, emotions, or feelings it conveys.
definitions.paper: Uncovering Argumentative Flow: A Question-Focus Discourse Structuring Framework

definitions.text: The observable behavior of linking a given image to one or more abstract semantic concepts based on perceived thematic or symbolic content.

relationships.paper: Uncovering Argumentative Flow: A Question-Focus Discourse Structuring Framework
relationships.evidence: To address these RQs, we present AbsVis, the first large-scale dataset of abstract concept attributions in images by humans and VLMs. (Section 1).
